sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a female who has been experiencing headaches for years, initially related to her menstrual periods but now occurring more frequently and severely.
She reports a pulsing sensation on her right side, behind her eye, and has noted an increase in frequency and severity of headaches with exercise.
She also reports a ringing in her ears and blurred vision on the side, which is intermittent.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness or tingling in her hands or feet or face is denied.
She does experience ringing in her ears and blurred vision on the side.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The funduscopic exam shows papilledema, which is swelling behind the eye.
The neurological exam is normal, with intact cranial nerves one through twelve, normal cranial reflex, and a normal gait.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is suspected to have idiopathic intracranial hypertension, a condition characterized by increased pressure in the brain without a known cause.
Problem list: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension; suspected.
Plan:
I ordered an MRI to check for any other conditions that could be contributing to her symptoms.
I also plan to perform a spinal tap to evaluate the pressure in the brain and to help alleviate symptoms.
I will refer the patient to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam.
I will also work with the patient's insurance company to obtain preapproval for the MRI.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient once the MRI and spinal tap results are available, and to schedule the neuro-ophthalmology appointment.
